prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were calculated for pyridine using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The analysis included finite-difference Fukui descriptors for nucleophilic (f⁻) and electrophilic (f⁺) attack sites, plus the dual descriptor. The most electrophilic site is the nitrogen atom (f⁺ = 0.48423), indicating strong preference for nucleophilic addition at N. The most nucleophilic site is ortho-carbon C4 (f⁻ = 0.11826), showing secondary reactivity. All per-atom Fukui indices, charges, and dual descriptors are reported in the results table. SCF converged with charge conservation passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000). Integrity status: OK, trustworthy. The calculation was a single point on the provided pyridine.xyz geometry without relaxation. A Fukui reactivity plot was generated and saved to the output directory.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
